entries.title: 1.9L Diesel

commonFailures: Boost leak from cracked intercooler pipe (whistling, power loss) at 80k+ miles
EGR valve coking causing limp mode
Dual-mass flywheel failure from 60,000 miles

commonProblems.h2: Volkswagen Sharan Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volkswagen Sharan's diesel engines dominate replacement enquiries, with the 1.9 TDI PD (BVK, AUY) and 2.0 TDI common rail (CFFA, CRVA) families most affected by age-related failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a Volkswagenmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Sharan.

problems.group: Boost Leak from Cracked Intercooler Pipe
problems.h4: Boost Leak from Cracked Intercooler Pipe - Why Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI Engines Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI Trendline, Comfortline, Sportline (1995-2010, BVK, AUY, ASZ codes)
problems.rootCause: Rubber intercooler hoses crack with age and heat cycling, causing boost pressure loss, whistling noise on acceleration, and reduced power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked intercooler pipe and clamps, pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: 2-4 years if other hoses are sound; inspect full system

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Hose replacement plus turbo actuator check, boost sensor calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses secondary damage; suitable if turbo is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt BVK/AUY unit with upgraded intercooler components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has 100k+ miles or other wear issues

problems.recommendation: On a 7M Sharan worth £1,500-£3,000, a £320 hose repair makes sense if the engine is otherwise healthy. However, if you're already experiencing high oil consumption or timing chain noise, investing in a rebuilt BVK engine from £1,200 often delivers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt BVK engine prices for your Volkswagen Sharan

problems.h4: 1.4 TSI Engine Knock & Power Loss - Carbon Buildup on Volkswagen Sharan 7N Petrol Models
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Sharan 1.4 TSI Comfortline, Highline (2010-2015, CAVA, CAVB codes)
problems.rootCause: Direct injection design causes carbon deposits on intake valves; combined with worn spark plugs at ~68,000km, this triggers knock sensor warnings and power reduction.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace spark plugs, walnut shell clean intake valves, reset adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; 30-40k mile improvement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Above plus timing chain tensioner inspection/replacement, oil pump check
repairOptions.longevity: Critical if rattle present; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CAVA unit with updated tensioner, carbon-resistant components
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if cylinder scoring or piston damage confirmed

problems.recommendation: If your Sharan shows knock warnings but no metallic rattle, the £390-£425 walnut blast and plug service is cost-effective. However, any cold-start chain rattle means the tensioner has failed - at that point, a rebuilt engine from £1,200 is often cheaper than the £1,350+ chain service on a high-mileage unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CAVA 1.4 TSI Volkswagen Sharan engine replacement

problems.h4: Fuel Injector Failure - Rough Running on Volkswagen Sharan 2.0 TDI Common Rail Engines
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Sharan 2.0 TDI CFFA, CFFB, CFGB (2008-2015, 7N generation)
problems.rootCause: Piezo injectors on common rail TDIs suffer internal electrical failure or nozzle coking, causing rough idle, misfires, and excessive fuel consumption.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if only one injector failed and mileage under 100k

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all four injectors, calibrate, update ECU software
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if two+ injectors failed or high-mileage unit

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CFFA unit with upgraded injectors, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if injector failure caused piston/cylinder damage

problems.recommendation: For a 7N Sharan with under 100k miles and one failed injector, the £515 specialist repair is sensible. But if multiple injectors have failed or you're over 120k miles, a rebuilt CFFA engine from £1,800 often costs less than replacing all four injectors at a main dealer.
problems.cta: Compare CFFA injector repair vs rebuilt engine costs for your Volkswagen Sharan

problems.h4: VR6 Timing Chain Guide Wear - Catastrophic Risk on Volkswagen Sharan 2.8L Engines
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Sharan 2.8 VR6 Exclusive Edition (1995-2005, AAA, AMY codes)
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guides become brittle with age and heat; once they crack, chain slack causes valve timing loss and potential piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove upper cover, visually inspect guides, advise on risk
repairOptions.longevity: Diagnostic only; does not fix the problem

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine front cover, replace guides/tensioner, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Complex job; only viable if no internal damage yet

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt AAA unit with metal guide upgrades, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Often cheaper than repair on high-mileage VR6; preserves collector value

problems.recommendation: If your VR6 Sharan has a cold-start rattle but still runs smoothly, act immediately - the £1,650+ chain service may save the engine. However, if the rattle is loud or the engine has missed timing, a rebuilt AAA engine from £2,200 is frequently more economical than attempting a repair on a damaged unit.
problems.cta: Get quotes for AAA VR6 Volkswagen Sharan engine replacement

problems.h4: Dual-Mass Flywheel Failure - Clutch Noise on Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI Manual Models
problems.affectedModels: Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI manual transmission variants (1995-2010, BVK, AUY codes)
problems.rootCause: DMF damping springs wear out from stop-start driving, causing rattling at idle, gear engagement shudder, and eventual clutch plate damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace clutch plate and release bearing only
repairOptions.longevity: Risky if DMF is worn; may need repeat job soon

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace dual-mass flywheel and full clutch kit, resurface flywheel face
Supply & fit rebuilt BVK unit with upgraded clutch components, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear issues or high mileage

problems.recommendation: On a healthy 1.9 TDI Sharan under 100k miles, the £1,100-£1,500 DMF+clutch service is the right choice. But if your engine also shows turbo wear, injector issues, or has over 120k miles, investing in a rebuilt BVK engine from £1,200 often delivers better long-term reliability than repairing individual components.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.9 TDI engine prices for your Volkswagen Sharan

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volkswagen Sharan
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with a whistling 1.9 TDI, a knocking 1.4 TSI, or injector issues on a 2.0 TDI, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volkswagen Sharan specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a Volkswagenmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volkswagen Sharan Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volkswagen Sharan and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a Volkswagenmum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Volkswagen Sharan Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volkswagen Sharan is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 1.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.9L TDI dominated the 7M Sharan (1995-2010) as the most popular and reliable diesel option, with PD injection from 2000 onwards improving refinement. These units remain the most common replacement enquiries due to age-related turbo and boost system failures.
commonFailurePoints: Boost leak from cracked intercooler pipe
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Sharan 1.9L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L TDI arrived in later 7M models (BKD PD) and became standard across the 7N generation (CFFA, CFFB, CFGB, CRVA common rail), offering improved power and emissions but introducing injector and DPF complexity.
commonFailurePoints: Piezo injector failure on common rail units
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Sharan 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L TSI (CAVA/CAVB) powered entry-level 7N Sharans (2010-2015), offering strong economy but suffering from EA111 timing chain tensioner issues that dominate replacement searches for this displacement.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causing cold-start rattle
carbon buildup on intake valves at 68k km
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Sharan 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 20V turbo (AJH, AUM, AGU) was the performance petrol choice for 7M Sharans, delivering 150hp with characteristic VW turbo responsiveness but coil pack and boost leak vulnerabilities.
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Sharan 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 2.0L 8V (ADY, ATM) provided simple, reliable power for base 7M Sharans, while the direct-injection 2.0L FSI (AXW) offered improved efficiency in later models but introduced carbon buildup concerns.
commonFailurePoints: Carbon buildup on FSI intake valves
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Sharan 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan 2.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The rare 2.8L VR6 (AAA, AMY) was the flagship petrol engine for 7M Sharans, delivering smooth V6 power and collector appeal, but timing chain guide wear remains a critical failure point requiring specialist attention.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic timing chain guide wear causing catastrophic failure risk
items.cta: Compare Volkswagen Sharan 2.8L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Volkswagen Sharan Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volkswagen Sharan engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early 1.9 TDI to the latest EA288 common rail.

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powers most UK Sharans across both generations, with 1.9L PD (7M) and 2.0L common rail (7N) dominating replacement enquiries due to high mileage and known failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel powers most UK Sharans across both generations, with 1.9L PD (7M) and 2.0L common rail (7N) dominating replacement enquiries due to high mileage and known failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Sharan Diesel Engine Families
families: BVK/AUY - 1.9L PD (7M), most common replacement code, boost leak prone
CFFA/CFFB - 2.0L common rail (7N early), injector failure concerns
CRVA - 2.0L EA288 (7N late), improved but AdBlue system issues
BKD - 2.0L PD (7M late), less common than 1.9L but similar failures
foundIn: 7M Generation (1995-2010) - BVK, AUY, ASZ, ANU, BKD
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability
Boost leak from cracked intercooler pipe on 1.9 TDI (whistling, power loss)
Piezo injector failure on 2.0 TDI common rail (£2,000+ repair)
High UK replacement demand on 116hp and 140hp variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Sharan Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Sharans offer simpler maintenance than diesel but face timing chain (1.4 TSI) and carbon buildup (FSI) issues that drive replacement searches for specific codes.
items.descriptor: Petrol Sharans offer simpler maintenance than diesel but face timing chain (1.4 TSI) and carbon buildup (FSI) issues that drive replacement searches for specific codes.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volkswagen Sharan Petrol Engine Families
families: CAVA/CAVB - 1.4L TSI (7N), timing chain tensioner failure common
AJH/AUM - 1.8L turbo (7M), coil pack and boost leak issues
AXW - 2.0L FSI (7M), carbon buildup on intake valves
AAA/AMY - 2.8L VR6 (7M), timing chain guide wear risk
foundIn: 7M Generation (1995-2010) - AJH, AUM, ADY, ATM, AXW, AAA, AMY
knownFor: Smoother, quieter operation than diesel equivalents
1.4 TSI timing chain rattle at cold start (EA111 weakness)
Carbon buildup on direct injection petrols causing knock warnings
VR6 units prized by collectors but require specialist rebuilders

items.title: Volkswagen Sharan Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Volkswagen Sharan was never offered with hybrid, plug-in hybrid or electric powertrains in the UK market across either generation.

engineYears.h2: Volkswagen Sharan Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Sharan's production run (1995-2022), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: Launch year introduced the durable 1.9L TDI with distributor pump injection and simple 2.0L petrol, establishing the Sharan's people-carrier credentials.
keyChanges: Volkswagen Sharan launched alongside Ford Galaxy and Seat Alhambra on shared platform
1.9L TDI 1Z (90hp) and 2.0L petrol ADY (115hp) as core engines
Manual transmission standard; automatic optional on higher trims
knownFor: Robust mechanicals with simple, repairable engines
Early TDI units lack PD refinement but are very durable
High rust susceptibility on sills and arches in UK climate
years.cta: Get quotes for 1995 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines

years.preview: 7M Facelift Prep: AFN TDI & VR6 Introduction
years.description: Mid-cycle updates added the refined AFN 1.9 TDI and the flagship 2.8L VR6, expanding appeal to performance and luxury buyers.
keyChanges: AFN 1.9 TDI (110hp) introduced with improved injection calibration
2.8L VR6 AAA (174hp) launched as range-topping performance option
Interior trim upgrades and additional safety equipment
knownFor: VR6 smoothness and sound highly valued by enthusiasts
AFN TDI offers good balance of power and economy
VR6 timing chain guides begin to show wear risk from 90k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 1997 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines
years.ticker: VR6 timing chain rattle • AFN injector seal leak • 2.0L petrol water pump

years.description: The switch to PD (Pumpe Düse) injection brought the BVK 1.9 TDI (116hp), which became the most common Sharan engine and replacement enquiry.
keyChanges: BVK 1.9 TDI PD (116hp) replaced AFN as volume diesel engine
1.8T 20V turbo petrol (AJH/AUM) added for performance petrol buyers
Facelift exterior styling and upgraded interior materials
knownFor: BVK becomes the definitive Sharan engine - reliable but boost leak prone
1.8T offers strong performance but coil pack failures common
DMF clutch wear emerges as key maintenance item from 60k miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines
years.ticker: BVK boost leak whistling • 1.8T coil pack misfire • DMF clutch rattle

years.description: Later 7M models introduced the 2.0L TDI BKD alongside the proven 1.9L, while petrol options remained unchanged as the generation neared end of production.
keyChanges: BKD 2.0 TDI PD (140hp) introduced as higher-power diesel option
Final trim updates: Pacific, United and Freestyle special editions
Production of 7M Sharan ended in 2010 after 15-year run
knownFor: BKD less common than 1.9L but shares similar failure patterns
High-mileage 7M units now dominant in used market (£1,500-£3,000 values)
Boost leak repairs (£320) often trigger replacement decisions on older units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines
years.ticker: 2.0 TDI injector seal • 1.9 TDI EGR valve • 1.8T turbo diverter

years.preview: 7N Launch: Common Rail Diesel & TSI Petrol
years.description: All-new 7N generation switched to common rail diesel (CFFA) and EA111 TSI petrol (CAVA), bringing modern efficiency but new failure modes.
keyChanges: New MQB-derived platform with updated styling and interior
CFFA 2.0 TDI common rail (140hp) replaced PD diesels
CAVA 1.4 TSI (150hp) introduced as efficient petrol option
2.0L FSI AXW carried over briefly for base petrol
mainEngines: CFFA/CFFB - 2.0L TDI common rail (Comfortline to Exclusive)
knownFor: Common rail refinement but piezo injector vulnerability (£2,000+ repair)
1.4 TSI timing chain tensioner issues dominate early 7N replacement searches
DPF and AdBlue complexity increases maintenance costs vs 7M
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines
years.ticker: CFFA injector failure • CAVA timing chain rattle • DPF blockage warning

years.description: Final update introduced the EA288 CRVA 2.0 TDI (150hp) with improved emissions, while 1.4 TSI was discontinued as petrol demand fell.
keyChanges: CRVA 2.0 TDI EA288 (150hp) replaced CFFA with updated emissions tech
1.4 TSI discontinued; no new petrol engines offered after 2015
Final special editions: Advantage, Cruise before production ended 2022
knownFor: CRVA more reliable than early common rail but AdBlue injector clogging emerges
Late 7N units command premium in used market due to lower mileage
Replacement demand now focused on injector and DPF-related failures
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015 Volkswagen Sharan replacement engines
years.ticker: CRVA AdBlue injector • DPF regeneration failure • EGR cooler leak

faq.h2: Volkswagen Sharan Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Can I fit a Ford Galaxy or Seat Alhambra engine in my Volkswagen Sharan?
items.answer: Yes, but only for 7M generation Sharans (1995-2010). The 7M Sharan shares its platform and engines with the Ford Galaxy and Seat Alhambra, so 1.9 TDI and 2.0 TDI engines are interchangeable. 7N models (2010+) do not share engines.
keyPoints: 7M Sharan (1995-2010) uses same engines as Galaxy/Alhambra
BVK, AUY, BKD diesel codes are direct fits across all three models
7N Sharan (2010+) has unique engines - no cross-compatibility
Always verify engine code and ECU compatibility before purchasing
items.cta: Compare compatible donor engines for your 7M Volkswagen Sharan

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Volkswagen Sharan engine in the UK?
items.answer: Used Sharan engines start from £400-£1,500, reconditioned units from £1,200-£2,800 depending on code. The common 1.9 TDI BVK averages £1,200-£1,800 rebuilt, while 2.0 TDI common rail (CFFA) runs £1,800-£2,800.
keyPoints: Used BVK 1.9 TDI from £950; reconditioned from £1,200
2.0 TDI common rail (CFFA/CRVA) £1,800-£3,200 rebuilt
VR6 AAA engines £2,200-£4,500 due to specialist demand
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 depending on location
items.cta: Get tailored Volkswagen Sharan engine replacement quotes today

items.question: Is it worth rebuilding my Volkswagen Sharan engine or should I replace it?
items.answer: For 7M Sharans valued under £3,000, a reconditioned engine from £1,200 often offers better value than repeated repairs. For 7N models or high-value variants, replacement with upgraded components extends life cost-effectively.
keyPoints: 7M Sharan values £1,500-£3,000 make £1,200 rebuilt engines sensible
Repeated £300-£500 repairs add up; one rebuilt unit solves multiple issues
7N models with higher values justify premium remanufactured options
All EngineMarket rebuilt units include 12-month warranty for peace of mind
items.cta: Compare rebuild vs replacement costs for your Volkswagen Sharan

items.question: Why is my Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI whistling and losing power?
items.answer: The most common cause is a cracked intercooler pipe causing a boost leak. This creates a whistling noise on acceleration, reduced power, and higher fuel consumption. Repair typically costs around £320 for parts and labour.
keyPoints: Cracked rubber intercooler hose is the usual culprit on BVK/AUY engines
Whistling noise under load is the classic symptom of boost loss
£320 repair is cost-effective if engine is otherwise healthy
Ignoring it can cause turbo over-speed and further damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Sharan 1.9 TDI boost leak repair

items.question: What causes engine knock on my Volkswagen Sharan 1.4 TSI?
items.answer: Carbon buildup on intake valves combined with worn spark plugs at around 68,000km triggers knock sensor warnings on the 1.4 TSI (CAVA/CAVB). A walnut blast clean and plug replacement costs £390-£425.
keyPoints: Direct injection design causes carbon deposits on intake valves
Knock warnings often appear between 60,000-85,000 miles
£390-£425 service resolves issue if caught before timing chain damage
Any cold-start metallic rattle indicates tensioner failure - act immediately
items.cta: Compare 1.4 TSI repair vs rebuilt engine costs for your Sharan

items.question: Are new Volkswagen Sharan engines still available from VW?
items.answer: New engines are only available for late 7N models (CRVA, CFFA) through Volkswagen, but at £4,000-£6,000 they are rarely cost-effective. The reconditioned market is the primary source for most Sharan owners.
keyPoints: VW supplies new engines only for 7N models still under warranty
7M engines (BVK, AUY) discontinued; reconditioned market is only option
Rebuilt engines from £1,200 offer similar longevity at fraction of cost
All EngineMarket units include 12-month warranty matching dealer terms
items.cta: Compare new vs reconditioned Volkswagen Sharan engine prices

items.question: How long does a Volkswagen Sharan engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical turnaround is 3-5 days for supply and fit, with same-week fitting available nationwide. Used engines may be dispatched in 2-3 days; rebuilt units require 3-5 days for final testing before shipping.
keyPoints: Used engine dispatch: 2-3 working days from order confirmation
Rebuilt/reconditioned units: 3-5 days for final bench testing
Specialist fitting typically completed within 1-2 days of engine arrival
Nationwide network means most UK locations covered within 48-hour transit
items.cta: Find Volkswagen Sharan engine supply & fit deals with fast turnaround

items.question: What warranty comes with a used or reconditioned Volkswagen Sharan engine?
items.answer: Used Sharan engines typically carry 3-6 month warranties, while reconditioned and rebuilt units include a Volkswagenmum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty when sourced through EngineMarket's vetted specialist network.
keyPoints: Used engines: 3-6 month warranty typical from UK breakers
Reconditioned/rebuilt: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour for engine-related failures
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Get quotes for Volkswagen Sharan engines with 12-month warranty

items.question: Can I convert my petrol Volkswagen Sharan to diesel?
items.answer: Not cost-effective. Converting petrol to diesel requires new fuel system, wiring loom, ECU, turbo, intercooler and exhaust, plus MoT reclassification. Buying a donor diesel Sharan is far cheaper and simpler.
keyPoints: Conversion costs £3,000-£5,000+ vs £1,200-£2,800 for direct replacement
MoT and insurance reclassification adds complexity and ongoing cost
Diesel donor Sharans widely available at similar values to petrol models
EngineMarket specialists can help source correct diesel replacement instead
items.cta: Compare diesel engine options for your Volkswagen Sharan variant

items.question: Where do I find the engine code on my Volkswagen Sharan?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block near the transmission bell housing. It also appears in your V5C registration document, service book, and on the engine bay VIN plate for quick verification.
keyPoints: Look on cylinder block near gearbox for stamped code (e.g., BVK, CFFA)
V5C document Section D.2 lists engine code and displacement
Service history and MOT certificates often record engine code
Enter registration on EngineMarket to auto-identify your exact engine
items.cta: Identify your Volkswagen Sharan engine code and get instant quotes
